summary: The four Register doors nobody picked up: B699 second witness (bundles table), corrected Euler product at bad primes, B302 commensurator route to asymmetric generations, and the two never-computed stale flags

d2.what_done: Built a joint-hypothesis table over once-punctured-torus bundles (the census sister-pairs m003/m004, m009/m010, m022/m023, m039/m040, plus three newly-constructed Twister mapping-tori at trace 6(alt), 7(primitive, non-cover), 8) using SnapPy 3.3.2 + Sage 10-ish (via `sage <script>.sage`, since plain-python SnapPy lacked Sage support for invariant_trace_field_gens/find_field) + PARI/GP (idealprimedec) in-seat. For each candidate: (a) verified fiberedness/monodromy trace via the EXACT Alexander polynomial (`M.alexander_polynomial()` in Sage-mode SnapPy) — a monic degree-2 Alexander poly a^2 - t*a + 1 is the char-poly of the genuine once-punctured-torus-bundle monodromy A in SL(2,Z), so this is a rigorous fiberedness+trace certificate, not a guess; (b) computed each manifold's invariant trace field exactly via `M.invariant_trace_field_gens().find_field(300 bits, degree≤6, optimize=True)`; (c) computed the EXACT splitting of the prime 5 in each field via PARI `idealprimedec(nfinit(poly),5)` (deterministic, symbolic ideal factorization — no numerics involved once the field poly is fixed); (d) computed monodromy discriminant t^2-4 directly from the trace via exact integer arithmetic (sympy). Also constructed three NEW once-punctured-torus bundles via `snappy.twister.Surface('S_1_1').bundle(monodromy=...)` (verified 1-cusped, positive volume ⇒ genuinely hyperbolic) to test cases the census sister-pairs don't cover: a second, non-covering trace-7 manifold (word R^5L, NOT the double cover (RL)^2 of m004 — confirmed non-isometric to that cover) to test whether "golden-flavored" monodromy disc (45=9·5) forces 5-inertness/Q(sqrt-3) independent of m004; a trace-8 manifold (R^3L^2); and a second, non-isometric trace-6 manifold (R^2L^2, distinct from census m039/m040's R^4L trace-6 realization) to test whether trace value alone fixes the field.
key_findings: FORCED/FIRM: m003 (the sister) is a genuine second witness for the NARROW original triple claim — verified Alexander poly a^2+3a+1 (monic, monodromy trace -3, fibered confirmed), invariant trace field x^2-x+1 = Q(sqrt-3) (disc -3, matches m004 exactly, as expected since m003/m004 are the textbook commensurable arithmetic pair over the Bianchi group for O_{-3} — RECALLED literature fact, cross-checked numerically), and 5 is exactly INERT there (idealprimedec: (e,f)=(1,2), exact PARI computation). Monodromy disc = (-3)^2-4 = 5 exactly, matching the golden signature. So the TRIPLE co-occurrence (5-inert ∧ fibered ∧ disc-5) DOES replicate at m003 — but this replication is FORCED by the known commensurability with m004 (same trace field is automatic for commensurable arithmetic manifolds), not an independent confirmation of the general law.

COUNTEREXAMPLE (repeated, ×5): m009/m010 (trace ±4, ITF=Q(sqrt-7) disc-7), m022/m023 (trace ±5, ITF=quartic disc 697), m039/m040 (trace ±6, ITF=cubic disc -44), and two newly-built bundles M32 (trace 8, ITF=quartic disc 3173) and M51 (trace 7, a genuinely DIFFERENT non-covering manifold from m004's double cover, ITF=degree-6 field disc -453683) are ALL exactly-verified fibered (monic-degree-2-Alexander-poly or Twister-constructed once-punctured-torus bundles) AND exactly-verified 5-INERT (in fact TOTALLY inert: idealprimedec gives a single prime of residue degree = field degree in every one of these 5 cases) — yet NONE has golden (disc-5) monodromy (their monodromy discs are 12, 21, 32, 60, 45 respectively, none equal to 5). This directly falsifies '5-inert ∧ fibered ⇒ golden(disc-5)' as a general implication: the antecedent is satisfied 6 times in this 7-manifold survey, the consequent only once (m003/m004, the same field/mechanism).

RESEMBLANCE/BASE-RATE FLAG: 5-inertness (often TOTAL inertness) turned out to be the MODAL behavior across this small sample of once-punctured-torus-bundle trace fields (6/7 cases), not a rare or special co-occurrence with fiberedness as B699's phrasing implied ('the surgery's real gift is manufacturing the 5-inert AND fibered [object] at all' reads as if that conjunction were special/rare) — the base rate here argues against treating 5-inert∧fibered as a distinguishing or golden-adjacent property; it appears to be closer to generic for this family. Caveat: n=7 is a small, non-uniformly-sampled set of words (mostly single-run R^p L^q forms), so this could partly reflect a selection effect in which words were easy to construct rather than a deep arithmetic law; a larger systematic sweep would strengthen the base-rate claim.

COUNTEREXAMPLE-SIDE CONFIRMATION: constructed a genuine 5-SPLIT fibered bundle — M22alt (word R^2L^2, trace 6, a DIFFERENT non-isometric manifold from the census m039/m040 realization of trace 6 via R^4L) has invariant trace field exactly Q(i) (disc -4), and 5 SPLITS completely there (idealprimedec: two primes, each (e,f)=(1,1) — matches the classical fact 5≡1 mod 4 splits in Q(i)). Its monodromy disc is 32 (not 5) — so it correctly LACKS golden structure, consistent with (though only one data point for) the converse direction of the hypothesis.

STRUCTURAL REFINEMENT: invariant trace field of a once-punctured-torus bundle is NOT a function of the bare monodromy trace alone. Two non-isometric trace-6 bundles were found with completely different fields: m039/m040 (word R^4L, ITF = cubic field disc -44, totally 5-inert) versus the newly built M22alt (word R^2L^2, ITF = Q(i) disc -4, 5-split). This means 'monodromy disc is bundle structure, 5-inert is field structure' (as stated in the task) is correct as a distinction, but the two are not even simply correlated through trace(A) — the field depends on the finer SL(2,Z)-conjugacy class / continued-fraction data of the specific word, not reducible to |t|.
No ramification of 5 was observed anywhere in the 7-manifold sample (all splitting types had e=1); this is a minor supporting data point, not independently significant given the small sample.
d2.outcome: COUNTEREXAMPLE-FOUND for the general joint-hypothesis law ("5-inert ∧ fibered ⇒ golden disc-5 monodromy") — refuted by 5 independent instances (m009/m010, m022/m023, m039/m040, and two newly-constructed bundles M32 trace-8 and M51 trace-7-non-cover) that are exactly-verified 5-inert AND fibered but lack golden(disc-5) monodromy structure. Within that overall refutation, m003 IS a genuine SECOND-WITNESS-CONFIRMS instance for the narrow original triple co-occurrence at trace ±3 specifically — but that replication is FORCED (a known consequence of m003/m004's commensurability, not independent new evidence), so it does not rescue the general law. Net: the joint hypothesis as a LAW is falsified; as a description of the single m004/m003 pair it holds (trivially/forced).
d2.base_rate_note: 5-inertness (often full/totally-inert Frobenius) occurred in 6 of the 7 sampled once-punctured-torus-bundle trace fields spanning degrees 2 through 6 — well above what a naive 'random prime, random field' base rate would predict for total inertia specifically in cubic/quartic/sextic fields (roughly 1/n for a degree-n field under generic Galois-group heuristics), and roughly at/above 50% for the quadratic cases. This argues against reading 5-inert∧fibered as a rare or golden-adjacent phenomenon; it appears closer to the generic/default behavior in this family. Caveat: n=7 with a non-uniform sampling of monodromy words (mostly simple R^pL^q forms chosen for constructibility, not a random/exhaustive sweep) means this could partly be a selection artifact rather than a deep law — flagged honestly rather than oversold.
d2.firewall_note: Gate 5 / Gate 5-Q respected throughout: all findings are stated as pure arithmetic-topology facts (fiberedness via Alexander polynomial, invariant trace field, prime splitting) with no Standard-Model value or physics label attached at any point; 'golden' and 'silver' are used purely as this project's own established shorthand for disc-5-type and Q(i)-type algebraic signatures (per the existing B699/B702 vocabulary already in the ledger), not as any physical interpretation.
d2.honest_gaps: Per THE LINDEP RULE: the m003/m004 field identification (Q(sqrt-3), disc -3) is FIRM — it is cross-validated by independent, well-established literature (m003/m004 are the textbook minimal-volume commensurable arithmetic pair over the Bianchi group for Q(sqrt-3); this is recalled domain knowledge, not verified from primary sources in this session) AND by the 300-bit-precision SnapPy/PARI find_field computation, which agree. The higher-degree field identifications (quartic disc 697 & 3173, cubic disc -44, degree-6 disc -453683) rest on SnapPy's standard find_field method (PSLQ/algdep-style identification at 300 bits precision, degree ≤6) — a widely used, reliable technique in the field, but NOT independently re-derived here from the raw gluing-variety/Neumann-Zagier equations, and I have no independent literature cross-check for these specific discriminants the way I do for m003/m004. This is flagged per the lindep rule as high-confidence-numerical rather than first-principles-symbolic. By contrast, the prime-5 SPLITTING computation for every field (idealprimedec via PARI) is fully exact and deterministic ONCE the defining polynomial is fixed — that step carries no numerical uncertainty. I did not attempt an exhaustive/systematic census sweep (e.g., scanning the full m00X-m0XX range programmatically) because a full-census volume-matching scan timed out (2 min) in this session; the 7-manifold sample here is representative but not exhaustive, which bears on the base-rate claim above. I also did not pin down a census name for the three Twister-constructed manifolds (M22alt, M32, M51) — they are treated as bona fide (verified 1-cusped, positive-volume, hence genuinely hyperbolic) manifolds in their own right rather than identified against the named census, which is sufficient for the counterexample argument but leaves a minor loose end for anyone wanting to cite them by census name later.

d3.what_done: Reconstructed a_n exactly from the banked B666-cell3 definition (frontier/B666_leads_campaign/cell3/cell3_landscape.py, PART B): a_n = (phi*chi0(n) + phi^(-2)*chi2(n))/2 for n>=3, chi0 = principal char mod 5, chi2 = Legendre symbol mod 5 ({0:0,1:1,2:-1,3:-1,4:1}) -- confirmed this equals the true trace-sequence value only for n>=3 (the GF's own correction terms show physical a_1=a_2=0, so those two indices are outside the sequence's domain and were excluded from all tests). Built a sympy-exact test harness (scratchpad scripts door3_euler.py/2/3/4) and: (1) reproduced B666's a_3*a_7 != a_21 witness exactly; (2) checked gcd(21,5): 21 is COPRIME to 5, so under the ACTUAL banked modulus (which is 5, not 15) B666's witness is already a good-prime case, not a bad-prime artifact; (3) exhaustively tested restricted multiplicativity a_m*a_n = a_mn for gcd(m,n)=1, m,n>=3, mn<=400, under three coprimality filters (coprime-to-5, coprime-to-15 per the task's literal hypothesis, and unrestricted) -- all three show numerous exact failures (sympy-verified, not float); (4) derived the general closed-form algebraic law for gcd(m,5)=gcd(n,5)=1 pairs: a_m*a_n - a_mn = 0 whenever chi2(m)=+1 or chi2(n)=+1, but equals the EXACT nonzero constant (1-sqrt5)/2 whenever chi2(m)=chi2(n)=-1 (both m,n are quadratic non-residues mod 5, i.e. m,n = 2 or 3 mod 5) -- proved symbolically with sympy over all four sign combinations, not just spot-checked.
key_findings: FORCED: the task's premise that the relevant modulus is 'level 15' with bad primes {3,5} does not match the banked B666-cell3 definition -- chi0, chi2 there are explicitly mod-5 characters (level 5, single bad prime 5); 'level 15' and its bad primes {3,5} belong to a DIFFERENT B666 object entirely (cellS's full hearing image im(rho) at level 15, B640) that has nothing to do with this generating function -- conflating the two was a citation/object-matching error in the Register, not a fact about the golden-stage GF.

OBSTRUCTED: under the correct (mod-5) modulus, gcd(21,5)=1, so B666's own a_3*a_7 vs a_21 witness is ALREADY a good-prime case, not a bad-prime one -- the premise that 'B666's kill actually only tested a bad-prime case' is FALSE; there is no bad-prime escape hatch to exploit.

FIRM (exact, sympy-verified): restricted multiplicativity a_m*a_n=a_mn FAILS even when both m,n are coprime to 5 (or to 15) and coprime to each other -- e.g. exact failures at (m,n)=(3,7)->21, (7,8)->56, and 71 more pairs found in a systematic scan to mn<=400 under the coprime-to-15 filter alone.

FIRM (exact algebraic law, proved by sympy over all sign cases, not numerics): for gcd(m,5)=gcd(n,5)=1, a_m*a_n=a_mn holds exactly IFF chi2(m)=+1 or chi2(n)=+1 (at least one a quadratic residue mod 5), and fails with the EXACT constant defect (1-sqrt5)/2 whenever chi2(m)=chi2(n)=-1. Since there are infinitely many primes in each residue class mod 5 (Dirichlet), this failure recurs at infinitely many good-prime pairs -- it cannot be patched by correcting local factors at any FINITE set of bad primes (3, 5, or otherwise).

STALE-CLOSING: the door as registered ('was the multiplicativity failure a bad-prime artifact fixable by modified local Euler factors at 3,5?') is answered definitively NO by direct exact computation, independent of and correcting the level-15/bad-prime{3,5} framing that motivated opening it.
d3.outcome: NO-PRODUCT — the door is CLOSED. Restricted multiplicativity away from any finite bad-prime set fails exactly (sympy-verified) and the failure is proved (not just observed) to recur infinitely often, via the closed-form defect a_m a_n - a_mn = (1-sqrt5)/2 whenever chi2(m)=chi2(n)=-1. No corrected Euler product (standard degree-2 factors at good primes, modified degree-1 factors at bad primes 3 and/or 5) can reproduce a_n for n<=200 or beyond; B666's refutation of a genuine Euler product stands, and is in fact stronger than the original witness suggested since the failure is generic rather than confined to bad primes. Additionally the task's own level-15/bad-primes-{3,5} hypothesis is factually corrected: the object's real modulus is 5 (single bad prime 5), and 21=3*7 is coprime to 5, so B666's original witness was already a good-prime case, not the bad-prime case the Register imagined.
d3.base_rate_note: A priori, an affine combination c1*chi_1(n) + c2*chi_2(n) of two DISTINCT Dirichlet characters (here the principal character and the Legendre symbol mod 5) with unequal, character-independent coefficients (phi/2 vs phi^-2/2) has no general reason to be multiplicative -- multiplicativity is a strong, non-generic algebraic property, and a random affine mixture of two multiplicative functions is essentially never itself multiplicative except in degenerate cases (e.g., one coefficient zero, or the two characters coincide). The a priori base rate that 'a natural-looking arithmetic sequence built this way happens to be multiplicative away from finitely many bad primes' is low; the exact computation here matches that expectation rather than overturning it -- this is a confirmatory result, not a surprising one, which lowers residual worry about an error in the negative finding.
d3.firewall_note: Gate 5-Q respected throughout: no SM value, no physics label was attached to a_n, chi0, chi2, or the Euler-product question -- this was treated purely as a number-theoretic/arithmetic-function question about a banked closed-form sequence and its Dirichlet-series structure. THE LINDEP RULE was honored: every claim of failure or success of a_m*a_n=a_mn was checked via sympy exact rational/radical arithmetic (sp.simplify/sp.nsimplify equality to zero), never floating-point comparison or numeric integer-relation guessing; the general algebraic law (chi2(m)=chi2(n)=-1 => exact defect (1-sqrt5)/2) was derived symbolically over all four +-1 sign combinations, not inferred from a finite numeric scan alone (the scan to n<=400 then serves as an independent corroboration of the closed-form law, not as the sole evidence).
d3.honest_gaps: Did not attempt to construct or rule out EVERY conceivable corrected-Euler-product ansatz (e.g., allowing the bad-prime local factors to be non-standard higher-degree, or allowing a twist/character shift of the whole product) -- the finding is that the SIMPLE restricted-multiplicativity hypothesis (a_m a_n = a_mn for m,n coprime to the modulus and to each other) fails, which is sufficient to close the specific door as registered (a genuine Euler product with corrected local factors only at finitely many bad primes requires exactly this restricted multiplicativity, so its failure is fully dispositive), but I did not explore exotic non-Euler-product 'motivic' structures that might still organize a_n (e.g. as coefficients of a Hilbert or half-integral-weight form with a different multiplicative convention) -- that remains genuinely open ground, just not what this door asked. Did not independently re-derive chi0/chi2/the closed form from first principles (i.e. did not re-run cell3_landscape.py or re-derive the SU(3)_2 trace construction) -- I took the banked closed-form definition as given (per the task's own instruction to 'reconstruct a_n exactly from the banked definition') and verified internal consistency (the a_1,a_2 correction-term reasoning) rather than re-deriving the trace formula from the group-theoretic construction myself.

d4.what_done:
Read frontier/B302_multiplicity_hidden_z3 (FINDINGS.md, multiplicity_hidden_z3.py, verdict.py) plus the surrounding cluster it cites or is cited by (B298, B307, B324, B325, B326, B734, B349) to pin exactly what B302 claims and why it survives the B298/B307 impossibility. Then computed/re-verified the first discriminating step: does the hidden Z/3 act on any matter-carrying object with three genuinely inequivalent orbits (the "asymmetric-generation shape" the door needs), across every candidate mechanism found or derivable. Independently re-ran the load-bearing algebra in-sandbox (sympy, exact ℤ[ω] arithmetic, no floats) rather than trusting the banked scripts' printed output.

B302 pinned: m004's knot group is torsion-free (no internal order-3; Sym(m004)=D4, order 8), but its commensurator (Neumann–Reid: m004 is arithmetic over ℚ(√−3)) is the Bianchi group PGL(2,O₋₃), which HAS order-3 elements (exhibited: g=[[0,−1],[1,−1]], g³=I; the Eisenstein unit ω, ω³=1) — because ℚ(√−3) carries extra (Eisenstein) units. m004 sits as an index-12 cover (Riley) of the minimal orbifold ℍ³/PGL(2,O₋₃) that carries this order-3 torsion. B302 explicitly does NOT claim this Z/3 IS the generation symmetry (physics dictionary, firewalled) — it only locates a hidden Z/3 in the relation (commensurability class), not the object.

Why B298/B307 do not kill this route (re-confirmed, FIRM): B298/B307 are theorems about a SINGLE hyperbolic knot's OWN invariant trace field — no hyperbolic knot's trace field can be Galois-cyclic (C₃, "symmetric triple") because Galois ⟹ totally real ⟹ no complex place, contradicting the hyperbolic geometric representation (always has a complex place). This is an INTERNAL algebraic obstruction on Γ's trace field. B302's Z/3 lives in Comm(Γ)=PGL(2,O₋₃), an EXTERNAL group acting on the ambient arithmetic lattice — a different mathematical object entirely, and in fact its absence from Γ itself (torsion-free) is the same fact underlying B298. So the escape is structurally valid, not a rules-lawyering dodge.

First discriminating step — tested every candidate "matter-carrying object with 3 orbits" I could find or derive:
key_findings: FIRM — B302's core claim re-verified in place: knot group torsion-free (Sym order 8, no order-3); commensurator PGL(2,O_-3) has an exact order-3 element g=[[0,-1],[1,-1]] (g^3=I, re-verified in sympy) and the Eisenstein unit ω (ω^3=1); this is a genuinely different object from Γ's own trace field, so B298/B307's single-object impossibility theorem does not apply to it — the escape from those theorems is valid, not a resemblance.

FORCED negative — the natural conjugation mechanism (B302/B324's own construction: three 'generations' as g^i-conjugates of the holonomy representation, gen_i = g^i·{a0,b0}·g^-i) is mechanically incapable of producing inequivalent copies. Re-verified exactly in-sandbox (sympy, ℤ[ω]): tr(a_i)=tr(b_i)=2 for i=0,1,2 identically, because conjugation always preserves trace (tr(PAP^-1)=tr(A)) — an elementary, unconditional linear-algebra fact, not a coincidence of this witness. Equal character across all three copies means they are mutually EQUIVALENT (conjugate) representations — the symmetric shape, the opposite of what the door needs. This is a structural obstruction to the whole conjugation-based family of constructions, not a resource limit.

RESEMBLANCE, not a real advance — decomposing the cross-trace overlap matrix M[i,j]=tr(a_i b_j^-1) (built from the three g-conjugates) into its three eigenspaces DOES give three formally distinct characters (re-verified exactly: eigenvalues 1, ω²=-0.5-0.866i (|·|=1), and 7-√3i (|·|=√52≈7.21), matching B324's banked numbers). But this three-way split is a generic consequence of Schur's lemma for ANY ℤ/3-linear action on ANY 3-dimensional space with distinct eigenvalues — it needs none of m004's specific arithmetic (not O_-3, not the index-12 cover, not the commensurator). It fails the cell's own base-rate-hard bar: a Z/3 splitting a 3-dim rep into 3 characters is cheap/universal, not object-specific.

FORCED negative — the coset-permutation candidate (derived this session, not previously banked as far as grep found): g of order 3 acting by left-multiplication on the 12 cosets of Γ in PSL(2,O_-3) (Riley's index-12 embedding) is provably fixed-point-free — if g fixed coset hΓ then h^-1gh∈Γ, an order-3 element of Γ, contradicting Γ torsion-free (the same fact underlying B298). Order-3 + no fixed points ⇒ every orbit has size exactly 3 ⇒ exactly 4 orbits of size 3. But size-3 orbits mean the three cosets IN each orbit are cyclically interchanged by g — symmetric/interchangeable within each triple, again the opposite of 'three inequivalent things.'

FORCED negative, different-object flag — B326 (already banked) computed a Z/3 acting on a matter-carrying arithmetic object (H₁=(ℤ/4)² of m004's 3-fold cyclic cover) via the companion matrix of Φ₃ mod 4, and found it IRREDUCIBLE mod 4 (and mod 2) — the module cannot split into three separately-labeled pieces at all ('cannot carry independent torsion labels'). This is suggestive but I flag explicitly: this Z/3 is the DECK GROUP of the canonical abelian 3-fold cyclic cover (from H₁(knot)=ℤ↠ℤ/3), which exists generically for every knot with infinite-cyclic H₁ — it is NOT shown identical to B302's hidden commensurator element g. Treating them as the same Z/3 (as B326's FINDINGS loosely implies by cross-referencing B302) would itself be an unverified resemblance; I did not find a proof they coincide.

Net: every candidate mechanism tested for 'the hidden Z/3 acts on a matter-carrying object with three inequivalent orbits' either fails by a forced structural fact (conjugation preserves trace; torsion-freeness forces fixed-point-free coset action; congruence torsion is irreducible) or only 'succeeds' via a generic representation-theory triviality unrelated to m004's specific arithmetic (Schur's-lemma eigenspace split). No genuinely object-specific, arithmetic-forced asymmetric-orbit mechanism was found or constructed.
d4.outcome: ROUTE-OBSTRUCTED (precisely scoped: B302's core claim — a genuine hidden ℤ/3 in the arithmetic commensurator PGL(2,O₋₃), correctly escaping B298/B307's single-object impossibility — stays FIRM/banked and is NOT what's obstructed. What is obstructed is the door's own next discriminating step: every computable candidate for that ℤ/3 acting on a matter-carrying object with three inequivalent orbits either is mechanically forced to give equivalent/symmetric copies, or gives inequivalent pieces only through a generic Schur's-lemma triviality that carries no m004-specific arithmetic content, or uses a different, unidentified Z/3 that is itself irreducible. No object-specific asymmetric-orbit realization was found.)
d4.base_rate_note: Two of the five candidates tested look superficially like a "pass" (three inequivalent things exist) but fail base-rate scrutiny on inspection: (1) the eigenspace-decomposition of a Z/3-invariant operator into 3 distinct characters is true for essentially any Z/3 acting linearly on any 3-dim space (Schur's lemma) — it requires none of m004's arithmetic, so finding it here is uninformative about this specific object; (2) "a Z/3 exists in the commensurator" is itself the thing the cell warns is generic ("a Z/3 exists somewhere is generic") — B302 already anticipated this by firewalling the generation-identification. The two genuinely forced/negative results (trace-invariance under conjugation; torsion-freeness forcing fixed-point-free coset orbits) are the informative ones, and both point away from the door, not toward it.
d4.firewall_note: Gate 5-Q held throughout: no SM value or physics label was asserted. Findings are stated structurally ("a matter-carrying representation," "three copies," "characters/eigenspaces," "orbits") rather than in terms of masses, Yukawas, or "the 27" — matching B302/B324's own fencing, which this cell's computation does not cross. The generation-identification of any Z/3 found here remains explicitly firewalled, exactly as B302/B324/B325 already state.
d4.honest_gaps: Did not exhaustively search for mechanisms beyond the five tested (conjugation, coset-permutation, generic eigenspace split, congruence-torsion deck action, and the abstract commensurator existence itself) — e.g., did not check genus/class-field theory (moot: Q(√−3) has class number 1, so no room for a class-group Z/3), did not run a full permutation-character computation of PSL(2,O_-3) on G/Γ via GAP/Magma (my coset-orbit conclusion rests on an elementary, exact group-theory proof, not a computational enumeration, so no tool gap there), and did not resolve whether B326's cyclic-cover deck Z/3 is provably identical to or independent of B302's commensurator element g — that ambiguity is flagged, not settled, and is a legitimate loose end in the banked frontier itself (B326's FINDINGS cross-references B302 without proving the identification). Did not verify B325/B326's underlying scripts by rerunning them (relied on reading their FINDINGS.md, which cite exact SnapPy/sympy computations); did independently rerun and exactly reproduce B302's and B324's core numeric claims.

DOOR3-EULER: NO-PRODUCT — CLOSED, very high confidence. DOOR4-COMMENSURATOR: ROUTE-OBSTRUCTED (not ROUTE-CLOSED) — medium-high confidence the 5 tested mechanisms all fail (2 forced structural negatives, 1 self-flagged generic Schur's-lemma triviality, 1 mechanism resting on an unresolved B326/B302 identity question), but the search was not exhaustive, so the door's actual question ('does an object-specific asymmetric-orbit mechanism exist') remains formally open, just with no positive candidate found yet. base_rate_risks: DOOR2: the 'n=7, 6-of-7 total-inert' modal-behavior claim rests on a small, non-uniformly sampled family (mostly simple R^pL^q monodromy words chosen because they were easy to construct with Twister, per the finding's own honest_gaps — a systematic census sweep timed out at 2 min and was never completed). DOOR4 (explicitly flagged risk area per the task): 'a Z/3 exists in the commensurator' is close to generic background, not a genuine discovery — Q(√−3) (with Q(i)) is one of only two imaginary quadratic fields carrying extra units, so PSL(2,O_-3) having elliptic (order-2/3) torsion is textbook Bianchi-orbifold structure, unrelated to anything specific about m004. DOOR4 secondary risk (already self-caught, worth reiterating): the eigenspace-decomposition-into-3-characters candidate is a generic Schur's-lemma consequence of ANY Z/3 acting linearly on ANY 3-dim space — it requires none of m004's arithmetic.
